evidence: The official PDF displays judges' names vertically, each on its own line with 5-space indentation (lines 48-52 of pdftotext output). The DB version incorrectly concatenates all names on a single line with spaces. The PDF rendering at 300dpi clearly shows the vertical stacking of judge names. This is a mechanical ingestion error where line breaks and indentation were lost.
note: Judicial signature lines in ND Supreme Court opinions consistently use this vertical, indented format. The DB version violates the court's established formatting convention for opinions.

source: /Users/jerod/refs/nd/opin/pdfs/2023/2023ND154.pdf page containing line 190 of source extract
evidence: PDF pdftotext extract shows 'Anderson v. Anderson, 368 N.W.2d 566, 569 (N.D. 1985).' at line 190. DB contains 39 repetitions of malformed '$Anderson\ v$ .' pattern (623 characters) instead. This is a catastrophic OCR artifact with no possible semantic interpretation—the repeating pattern cannot be intentional court text.
note: CRITICAL DEFECT: 623-character block of garbage that should be a single citation. Appears to be OCR markup corruption where citation was scanned multiple times or OCR engine failed to resolve a scanning artifact.

evidence: Standard ND Supreme Court citation format is '2023 ND 154' (the reporter abbreviation is 'ND'). PDF line 12 shows '2023 ND 154'. DB shows corrupted 'IN 1)' where 'ND' should be, consistent with OCR misreading 'ND' as 'IN' and '.' as '1)'.
note: Occurs in caption line of opinion. Digit/letter confusion in OCR (N→I, D→1), period→parenthesis.

evidence: PDF line 18 shows lowercase 'v.' (the standard case separator abbreviation in legal captions). DB shows uppercase 'V.' Lowercase to uppercase confusion is a known OCR defect.
note: Legal convention requires lowercase 'v.' to separate parties in case names. Uppercase 'V.' violates standard caption formatting.

evidence: PDF shows compact ellipsis character (U+2026, rendered as …) on line 329 between 'I am.' and 'Q.' with proper line breaks. DB shows malformed spaced ellipsis '. . . . .' and drops the line breaks, inserting instead '- Q.' on same line.
note: Ingestion error: proper Unicode ellipsis U+2026 converted to spaced dots, and line structure lost. This occurs in trial testimony transcript within [¶20] section.

evidence: DB lines 3-4 show '2020 * T n -' and '2023 IN 1) 154' as separate lines. PDF shows only '2023 ND 154' on line 12. The '2020 * T n -' line does not exist in the source and appears to be spurious OCR output (possibly attempting to read '2023 ND' with scanning artifacts).
note: Noise artifact: this line has no counterpart in the PDF and is nonsensical ('2020 * T n -'). Should be deleted entirely. Likely OCR attempt to read a smudge or fold mark.

evidence: PDF shows 'affirm.' without Roman numeral I. The I is a section header that appears on its own line in the PDF (page 2) and should not be attached to the preceding sentence.
note: Roman numeral section header incorrectly merged with end of [¶1]

evidence: PDF shows citation ending with ').' (followed by page break and section II), not with ').' followed by Roman numeral III. The III appears as standalone section header on next page.
note: Roman numeral section header III incorrectly appended to citation at end of [¶5]

evidence: PDF shows period only: 'not all purposes.' The IV is a standalone section header on next page, not part of text.
note: Roman numeral section header IV incorrectly appended to end of [¶14]

evidence: PDF shows 'was ineffective.' (period) at end of first part of opinion. The V is standalone section header for McEvers concurring opinion, not part of main text.
